raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California State Assembly passed Assembly Bill 280 to improve the accuracy of health plan provider directories.",
    "AB 280 sets enforceable benchmarks for directory accuracy starting at 60 percent and increasing to 95 percent by 2029.",
    "AB 280 imposes escalating financial penalties for noncompliance with directory accuracy standards.",
    "The bill requires health plans to arrange patient care and cover out-of-network costs if patients rely on inaccurate provider listings.",
    "AB 280 mandates the use of a centralized database to streamline updates of provider directories.",
    "Health plans are required to annually verify provider directory information in compliance with specified standards.",
    "Inaccurate health plan directories disproportionately harm vulnerable communities, including those with limited English proficiency and people with disabilities."
  ]
}
```
